Object.Name: PlatformConfiguration
Object.Description1:
This LwM2M Object configures GEISA Platform behavior for local monitoring, reporting, logging, queueing, and app-message handling.
This Object is single-instance per edge device. It represents desired and effective behavior of the GEISA Platform for one edge device and is intended for operator or EMS-visible configuration state rather than current operational health.
This Object does not replace native LwM2M configuration for firmware, application lifecycle, cellular, APN, WLAN, bearer selection, remote SIM, or QoS. It does not carry app-domain configuration payloads; app-domain configuration uses the GEISA App Messaging Object. It does not report current platform health; the GEISA Platform Monitoring Object reports current platform health, state, and counters. It does not store durable logs or event records; those remain in the Event Log Object.

Item.Name: Configuration Revision
Item.Description: Platform-generated revision identifier for the current GEISA Platform configuration set represented by this Object Instance. This value is intended for audit, synchronization, and troubleshooting.

Item.Name: Configuration State
Item.Description: Current relationship between the desired GEISA Platform configuration and the effective configuration currently exposed by the platform. 0 = Unknown 1 = Effective configuration matches desired configuration 2 = Pending application 3 = Partially applied 4 = Desired value adjusted by platform policy 5 = Rejected 6 = Reverted to previous effective configuration 7..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific states

Item.Name: Last Desired Configuration Update Time
Item.Description: Time at which the desired GEISA Platform configuration was most recently requested, received, or revised. This timestamp reflects the desired configuration input, not necessarily the time at which the configuration became effective. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. When the timestamp is presented or interpreted in platform-local operational context, use /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Effective Configuration Update Time
Item.Description: The last time at which the effective GEISA Platform configuration was updated. This timestamp reflects when the platform accepted, normalized, or applied configuration values into the effective configuration exposed by this object. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. When the timestamp is presented or interpreted in platform-local operational context, use /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Configuration Rejection Time
Item.Description: Time at which a desired GEISA Platform configuration was most recently rejected. This timestamp reflects the rejection decision time, not the time at which the desired configuration was originally requested. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. When the timestamp is presented or interpreted in platform-local operational context, use /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Configuration Rejection Reason
Item.Description:
Reason for the most recent configuration rejection.
0 = None 1 = Unknown 2 = Unsupported value 3 = Value outside supported range 4 = Policy denied 5 = Insufficient resources 6 = Temporarily unavailable 7 = Requires restart or safe state 8 = Conflicts with another configuration 9 = Security restriction 10..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined reasons 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific reasons

Item.Name: Last Configuration Rejection Detail
Item.Description: Short platform-generated detail associated with the most recent configuration rejection.

Item.Name: Desired Default Sampling Period
Item.Description:
Desired platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds used when a more specific GEISA Platform cadence is not configured.
This value controls how often the platform collects, refreshes, computes, or reports this class of operational data. It does not require the monitored OMA object to carry its own interval.

Item.Name: Effective Default Sampling Period
Item.Description:
Effective platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds currently used when a more specific GEISA Platform cadence is not configured.
This value controls how often the platform collects, refreshes, computes, or reports this class of operational data. It does not require the monitored OMA object to carry its own interval.

Item.Name: Desired Host Sampling Period
Item.Description: Desired platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds for host or device resource monitoring associated with GEISA platform behavior.

Item.Name: Effective Host Sampling Period
Item.Description: Effective platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds currently used for host or device resource monitoring associated with GEISA platform behavior.

Item.Name: Desired Platform Sampling Period
Item.Description: Desired platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds for GEISA Platform monitoring and internal platform state collection.

Item.Name: Effective Platform Sampling Period
Item.Description: Effective platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds currently used for GEISA Platform monitoring and internal platform state collection.

Item.Name: Desired App Monitoring Sampling Period
Item.Description: Desired platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds for GEISA App Monitoring data collection.

Item.Name: Effective App Monitoring Sampling Period
Item.Description: Effective platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds currently used for GEISA App Monitoring data collection.

Item.Name: Desired App Accounting Sampling Period
Item.Description:
Desired platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds for GEISA App Accounting data collection and refresh.
This cadence controls how often accounting state is collected, recomputed, or reported. It is distinct from the app accounting collection period represented by resources 90 and 91 in this object.

Item.Name: Effective App Accounting Sampling Period
Item.Description:
Effective platform-side sampling or update cadence in seconds currently used for GEISA App Accounting data collection and refresh.
This cadence controls how often accounting state is collected, recomputed, or reported. It is distinct from the app accounting collection period represented by resources 90 and 91 in this object.

Item.Name: Desired Default Monitoring Window
Item.Description:
Desired aggregation or evaluation window in seconds used when a more specific GEISA Platform monitoring window is not configured.
This window controls over what duration samples are evaluated for derived values, alerting, health, or upstream update decisions.

Item.Name: Effective Default Monitoring Window
Item.Description:
Effective aggregation or evaluation window in seconds currently used when a more specific GEISA Platform monitoring window is not configured.
This window controls over what duration samples are evaluated for derived values, alerting, health, or upstream update decisions.

Item.Name: Desired Host Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Desired aggregation or evaluation window in seconds for host or device resource monitoring associated with GEISA platform behavior.

Item.Name: Effective Host Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Effective aggregation or evaluation window in seconds currently used for host or device resource monitoring associated with GEISA platform behavior.

Item.Name: Desired Platform Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Desired aggregation or evaluation window in seconds for GEISA Platform monitoring and derived platform state evaluation.

Item.Name: Effective Platform Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Effective aggregation or evaluation window in seconds currently used for GEISA Platform monitoring and derived platform state evaluation.

Item.Name: Desired App Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Desired aggregation or evaluation window in seconds for GEISA App Monitoring aggregation and derived state evaluation.

Item.Name: Effective App Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Effective aggregation or evaluation window in seconds currently used for GEISA App Monitoring aggregation and derived state evaluation.

Item.Name: Desired Minimum Upstream Severity
Item.Description:
Desired minimum severity threshold for upstream reporting.
0 = Unknown 1 = Informational 2 = Warning 3 = Minor 4 = Major 5 = Critical 6..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined severities 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific severities

Item.Name: Effective Minimum Upstream Severity
Item.Description:
Effective minimum severity threshold currently used for upstream reporting.
0 = Unknown 1 = Informational 2 = Warning 3 = Minor 4 = Major 5 = Critical 6..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined severities 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific severities

Item.Name: Desired Event Suppression Period
Item.Description: Desired suppression period in seconds used to limit repeated reporting of equivalent events or alarms.

Item.Name: Effective Event Suppression Period
Item.Description: Effective suppression period in seconds currently used to limit repeated reporting of equivalent events or alarms.

Item.Name: Desired Clear/Recovery Reporting Enabled
Item.Description: Desired setting indicating whether clear or recovery events should be reported upstream when a previously reported condition returns to normal.

Item.Name: Effective Clear/Recovery Reporting Enabled
Item.Description: Effective setting currently used to determine whether clear or recovery events are reported upstream when a previously reported condition returns to normal.

Item.Name: Desired Clear Condition Duration
Item.Description: Desired duration in seconds that a condition should remain clear before the platform reports a clear or recovery event.

Item.Name: Effective Clear Condition Duration
Item.Description: Effective duration in seconds currently used before the platform reports a clear or recovery event.

Item.Name: Desired Default Reporting Priority
Item.Description:
Desired default reporting priority used when a more specific message or event priority is not supplied.
0 = Unknown 1 = Urgent 2 = Immediate 3 = Best effort 4 = Latest 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined priorities 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific priorities

Item.Name: Effective Default Reporting Priority
Item.Description:
Effective default reporting priority currently used when a more specific message or event priority is not supplied.
0 = Unknown 1 = Urgent 2 = Immediate 3 = Best effort 4 = Latest 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined priorities 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific priorities

Item.Name: Desired Platform Log Level
Item.Description:
Desired log level for GEISA Platform services and components.
0 = Unknown 1 = Off 2 = Error 3 = Warning 4 = Info 5 = Debug 6 = Trace 7..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined log levels 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific log levels

Item.Name: Effective Platform Log Level
Item.Description:
Effective log level currently used for GEISA Platform services and components.
0 = Unknown 1 = Off 2 = Error 3 = Warning 4 = Info 5 = Debug 6 = Trace 7..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined log levels 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific log levels

Item.Name: Desired App Log Level Default
Item.Description:
Desired default log level applied to GEISA applications when a more specific application log configuration is not provided.
0 = Unknown 1 = Off 2 = Error 3 = Warning 4 = Info 5 = Debug 6 = Trace 7..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined log levels 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific log levels

Item.Name: Effective App Log Level Default
Item.Description:
Effective default log level currently applied to GEISA applications when a more specific application log configuration is not provided.
0 = Unknown 1 = Off 2 = Error 3 = Warning 4 = Info 5 = Debug 6 = Trace 7..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined log levels 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific log levels

Item.Name: Desired Event Log Retention Count
Item.Description: Desired maximum retained event or log record count for GEISA-managed durable event storage. A value of 0 indicates that no count limit is configured or that retention is unlimited according to platform policy.

Item.Name: Effective Event Log Retention Count
Item.Description: Effective maximum retained event or log record count currently used for GEISA-managed durable event storage. A value of 0 indicates that no count limit is configured or that retention is unlimited according to platform policy.

Item.Name: Desired Audit Logging Enabled
Item.Description: Desired setting indicating whether audit logging is enabled for GEISA Platform administrative or security-relevant actions.

Item.Name: Effective Audit Logging Enabled
Item.Description: Effective setting currently used to determine whether audit logging is enabled for GEISA Platform administrative or security-relevant actions.

Item.Name: Desired Remote Logging Enabled
Item.Description: Desired flag indicating whether platform-side remote logging is enabled.

Item.Name: Effective Remote Logging Enabled
Item.Description: Effective flag indicating whether platform-side remote logging is enabled.

Item.Name: Desired Remote Log Server
Item.Description: Desired URI of the remote log collector or event sink used by platform remote logging.

Item.Name: Effective Remote Log Server
Item.Description: Effective URI of the remote log collector or event sink used by platform remote logging.

Item.Name: Desired Remote Log Severity Threshold
Item.Description:
Desired minimum severity threshold for remote logging.
0 = Emergency 1 = Alert 2 = Critical 3 = Error 4 = Warning 5 = Notice 6 = Informational 7 = Debug

Item.Name: Effective Remote Log Severity Threshold
Item.Description:
Effective minimum severity threshold for remote logging.
0 = Emergency 1 = Alert 2 = Critical 3 = Error 4 = Warning 5 = Notice 6 = Informational 7 = Debug

Item.Name: Desired Remote Log Policy Reference
Item.Description: Desired reference or identifier for the remote logging policy currently associated with the platform.

Item.Name: Effective Remote Log Policy Reference
Item.Description: Effective reference or identifier for the remote logging policy currently associated with the platform.

Item.Name: Desired Remote Log Transport Security Profile
Item.Description: Desired transport security profile used for platform remote logging.

Item.Name: Effective Remote Log Transport Security Profile
Item.Description: Effective transport security profile used for platform remote logging.

Item.Name: Desired Upstream Queue Enabled
Item.Description: Desired setting indicating whether the GEISA Platform should buffer outbound upstream messages in a queue or spool when immediate transmission is not possible or not preferred.

Item.Name: Effective Upstream Queue Enabled
Item.Description: Effective setting currently used to determine whether the GEISA Platform buffers outbound upstream messages in a queue or spool.

Item.Name: Desired Max Queued Messages
Item.Description: Desired maximum number of messages that the upstream queue or spool should retain.

Item.Name: Effective Max Queued Messages
Item.Description: Effective maximum number of messages currently retained by the upstream queue or spool.

Item.Name: Desired Max Queued Bytes
Item.Description: Desired maximum queued or spooled payload volume in bytes for upstream buffering.

Item.Name: Effective Max Queued Bytes
Item.Description: Effective maximum queued or spooled payload volume in bytes currently used for upstream buffering.

Item.Name: Desired Queue TTL
Item.Description: Desired time-to-live in seconds for queued or spooled messages before expiration. This value applies to messages retained in the platform queue or spool and may be derived from, or constrained by, the platform default message TTL when a more specific queue policy is not configured.

Item.Name: Effective Queue TTL
Item.Description: Effective time-to-live in seconds currently used for queued or spooled messages before expiration. This value reflects the platform-accepted queue or spool TTL after validation, policy limits, fallback handling, or default message TTL constraints have been applied.

Item.Name: Desired Queue Drop Policy
Item.Description:
Desired queue or spool drop policy used when buffering limits are reached.
0 = Unknown 1 = Reject new messages 2 = Drop oldest 3 = Drop newest 4 = Drop lowest priority 5 = Coalesce where possible 6..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined drop policies 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific drop policies

Item.Name: Effective Queue Drop Policy
Item.Description:
Effective queue or spool drop policy currently used when buffering limits are reached.
0 = Unknown 1 = Reject new messages 2 = Drop oldest 3 = Drop newest 4 = Drop lowest priority 5 = Coalesce where possible 6..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined drop policies 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific drop policies

Item.Name: Desired Queue Coalescing Enabled
Item.Description: Desired setting indicating whether the GEISA Platform may collapse eligible queued upstream messages before transmission or storage. When enabled, the platform can reduce redundant or stale queued updates according to the configured queue coalescing policy, such as keeping the latest state update while preserving events or alarms that must be delivered individually.

Item.Name: Effective Queue Coalescing Enabled
Item.Description: Effective setting indicating whether the GEISA Platform is currently allowed to collapse eligible queued upstream messages before transmission or storage. This value reflects the platform-accepted behavior after validation, policy limits, or fallback handling have been applied.

Item.Name: Desired Queue Coalescing Policy
Item.Description:
Desired policy for how the GEISA Platform should collapse eligible queued upstream messages when queue coalescing is enabled. The policy determines which queued messages may be combined, replaced, summarized, or preserved.
0 = Unknown 1 = Disabled 2 = By app and message type 3 = By app, message type, and subject 4 = By explicit coalescing key 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined coalescing policies 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific coalescing policies

Item.Name: Effective Queue Coalescing Policy
Item.Description:
Effective policy used by the GEISA Platform to collapse eligible queued upstream messages when queue coalescing is enabled. This value reflects the platform-accepted policy after validation, policy limits, or fallback handling have been applied.
0 = Unknown 1 = Disabled 2 = By app and message type 3 = By app, message type, and subject 4 = By explicit coalescing key 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined coalescing policies 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific coalescing policies

Item.Name: Desired Default Message TTL
Item.Description: Desired default time-to-live in seconds for messages buffered or handled by the GEISA Platform when a more specific TTL is not provided.

Item.Name: Effective Default Message TTL
Item.Description: Effective default time-to-live in seconds currently used for messages buffered or handled by the GEISA Platform when a more specific TTL is not provided.

Item.Name: Desired Max App Message Payload Size
Item.Description: Desired maximum GEISA application message payload size in bytes accepted by the platform before local rejection, truncation, or alternate handling according to platform policy.

Item.Name: Effective Max App Message Payload Size
Item.Description: Effective maximum GEISA application message payload size in bytes currently accepted by the platform before local rejection, truncation, or alternate handling according to platform policy.

Item.Name: Desired App Message Priority Policy Reference
Item.Description: Desired platform-defined policy reference currently used to map, adjust, or constrain GEISA application message priorities before local handling or upstream reporting. The reference may identify a local policy name, policy version, URI, or other operator-defined policy identifier.

Item.Name: Effective App Message Priority Policy Reference
Item.Description: Effective platform-defined policy reference currently used to map, adjust, or constrain GEISA application message priorities before local handling or upstream reporting. The reference may identify a local policy name, policy version, URI, or other operator-defined policy identifier.

Item.Name: Desired App Message Routing Policy Reference
Item.Description: Desired platform-defined policy reference used to select how GEISA application messages are routed, constrained, or handled before local processing or upstream reporting. The reference may identify a local policy name, policy version, URI, or other operator-defined policy identifier.

Item.Name: Effective App Message Routing Policy Reference
Item.Description: Effective platform-defined policy reference currently used to route, constrain, or handle GEISA application messages before local processing or upstream reporting. The reference may identify a local policy name, policy version, URI, or other operator-defined policy identifier.

Item.Name: Desired QoS Policy Reference
Item.Description: Desired reference or identifier for the QoS policy associated with GEISA Platform behavior. Detailed bearer or flow QoS configuration remains in Object 10540 and related network or bearer objects.

Item.Name: Effective QoS Policy Reference
Item.Description: Effective reference or identifier for the QoS policy currently associated with GEISA Platform behavior. Detailed bearer or flow QoS configuration remains in Object 10540 and related network or bearer objects.

Item.Name: Desired App Accounting Collection Period
Item.Description: Desired accounting collection period in seconds used to represent the app accounting counter window for the platform.

Item.Name: Effective App Accounting Collection Period
Item.Description: Effective accounting collection period in seconds currently used to represent the app accounting counter window for the platform.

Item.Name: Desired App Accounting Window Types
Item.Description:
Desired bitmask of supported app accounting window types.
0 = Unspecified / implementation default 1 = Configured collection period 2 = Daily 4 = Since reboot 8 = Rolling window 16 = Lifetime 32 = Operator-defined Higher powers of two are reserved for future window types.

Item.Name: Effective App Accounting Window Types
Item.Description:
Effective bitmask of supported app accounting window types.
0 = Unspecified / implementation default 1 = Configured collection period 2 = Daily 4 = Since reboot 8 = Rolling window 16 = Lifetime 32 = Operator-defined Higher powers of two are reserved for future window type expansion.

Item.Name: Desired App Accounting Rolling Window Duration
Item.Description: Desired rolling-window duration in seconds for app accounting.

Item.Name: Effective App Accounting Rolling Window Duration
Item.Description: Effective rolling-window duration in seconds currently used for app accounting.

Item.Name: Desired App Accounting Retention Count
Item.Description: Desired retained history count for app accounting window instances or snapshots.

Item.Name: Effective App Accounting Retention Count
Item.Description: Effective retained history count for app accounting window instances or snapshots.

Item.Name: Desired App Accounting Policy Reference
Item.Description: Desired reference or identifier for the app accounting policy currently associated with the platform.

Item.Name: Effective App Accounting Policy Reference
Item.Description: Effective reference or identifier for the app accounting policy currently associated with the platform.

Item.Name: Per-App Accounting Overrides Enabled
Item.Description: Desired flag indicating whether per-app accounting policy overrides are permitted in future runtime behavior.

Item.Name: Effective Per-App Accounting Overrides Enabled
Item.Description: Effective flag indicating whether per-app accounting policy overrides are permitted in future runtime behavior.

Item.Name: Desired Default App Accounting Maximum Bytes
Item.Description: Desired maximum bytes allowed during the accounting collection period. A value of zero means unlimited.

Item.Name: Effective Default App Accounting Maximum Bytes
Item.Description: Effective maximum bytes currently allowed during the accounting collection period. A value of zero means unlimited.

Item.Name: Desired Default App Accounting Message Limit
Item.Description: Desired maximum number of messages allowed during the accounting collection period. A value of zero means unlimited.

Item.Name: Effective Default App Accounting Message Limit
Item.Description: Effective maximum number of messages currently allowed during the accounting collection period. A value of zero means unlimited.

Item.Name: Desired Default App Accounting Throttle Message Limit
Item.Description: Desired message limit used by throttling policy during the throttle period. A value of zero means unlimited.

Item.Name: Effective Default App Accounting Throttle Message Limit
Item.Description: Effective message limit currently used by throttling policy during the throttle period. A value of zero means unlimited.

Item.Name: Desired Default App Accounting Throttle Period
Item.Description: Desired throttle period in seconds. A value of zero disables the throttle period.

Item.Name: Effective Default App Accounting Throttle Period
Item.Description: Effective throttle period in seconds. A value of zero disables the throttle period.

Item.Name: Desired Quota Exhaustion Action
Item.Description:
Desired local platform action to take when an accounting quota or limit is exhausted. Upstream ADM/EMS may observe, alert, or enforce related behavior, but this value describes the local platform action only.
0 = Unknown 1 = No local enforcement 2 = Report only 3 = Degrade priority 4 = Throttle 5 = Reject new work 6 = Drop new work 7 = Block communication 8..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined actions 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific actions

Item.Name: Effective Quota Exhaustion Action
Item.Description:
Effective local platform action currently taken when an accounting quota or limit is exhausted. Upstream ADM/EMS may observe, alert, or enforce related behavior, but this value describes the effective local platform action only.
0 = Unknown 1 = No local enforcement 2 = Report only 3 = Degrade priority 4 = Throttle 5 = Reject new work 6 = Drop new work 7 = Block communication 8..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined actions 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific actions

Item.Name: Desired Accounting Enforcement Policy Reference
Item.Description: Desired reference or identifier for the accounting enforcement policy currently associated with the platform.

Item.Name: Effective Accounting Enforcement Policy Reference
Item.Description: Effective reference or identifier for the accounting enforcement policy currently associated with the platform.
